aluma: Could preserve project-intel research and outreach workflows if self-hosting/privacy checks pass.
next: Verify privacy model and self-hosting path before storing anything sensitive; start with non-sensitive prompt/workflow archives.
name: Memoriq
summary: Encrypted vault and Chrome extension for saving, searching, organizing, exporting, and importing useful ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, and Grok conversations.
drprepper: Useful pattern for preserving AI research, SOP drafts, pricing decisions, troubleshooting sessions, and prompt libraries in a searchable place.

name: Viktor Oddy — Claude Design full course
summary: One-hour course/reference for building interactive animated websites with Claude-style workflows.
drprepper: Useful if we create a conversion-focused animated fulfillment page without making it feel like a gimmick.
aluma: Could support polished sales-engineering explainers for formwork/shoring concepts.
next: Extract workflow notes before using it on a production-facing page.

aluma: Useful pattern for guarded lead-gen, outreach, reporting, and Sheets/CRM automations where every API action needs an allowlist and traceable audit trail.
next: Test only on a sandbox integration first; require dry-run mode, allowlisted non-destructive calls, audit logs, and human approval before any production write action.
name: Swytchcode CLI
summary: CLI and control layer for letting AI agents call production APIs with auth handling, retries, idempotency, policy controls, and audit logs across a large API catalog.
drprepper: Strong fit for safer internal automation around CRM updates, billing/admin tasks, email/reporting workflows, and future PrepShip/client-portal tool calls without giving agents unchecked production access.

aluma: Could support structured construction prospecting notes or proposal outlines if privacy boundaries are clear and no confidential project data is stored there.
next: Use only with non-sensitive test documents first; verify persistence/privacy behavior and MCP access before making it part of any regular workflow.
name: ODocs.co
summary: Free, no-login collaborative docs and draw.io-style workspace with REST and MCP access so humans and AI agents can read and edit the same working document.
drprepper: Could be a lightweight scratchpad for non-sensitive SOP drafts, project specs, lead-research templates, and task notes shared with AI workers.

name: Viktor Oddy — renewable hero reference
summary: Clean nature/tech animated hero page reference built around a premium visual metaphor.
drprepper: Adapt the structure, not the theme: floating warehouse/order-flow diorama instead of renewable energy imagery.
aluma: Possible visual metaphor for jobsite shoring systems or project lifecycle.
next: Use as visual reference if we prototype an animated DR Prepper hero.

aluma: Useful for construction prospecting and public directory workflows if scraping rules, rate limits, and source quality are reviewed first.
next: Test only on public or sandbox pages first; inspect generated scripts for credentials, rate limits, and terms-of-service risk before using on any supplier/customer portal.
name: AutomatiQ
summary: Open-source tool that watches a user browse a website, then generates HTTP-based automation or scraper scripts for sites without clean APIs.
drprepper: Strong pattern for automating repetitive portal work: supplier dashboards, carrier portals, lead-source pages, inventory checks, and marketplace admin where APIs are missing.

aluma: Could help Aluma improve project-intel or contractor lead pages by tracking competitor pages, content gaps, and visitor intent before changing outreach pages.
next: Verify Product Hunt launch/pricing details outside the cron environment, then test only on a non-critical page with analytics, human approval, and rollback rules before letting agents publish changes.
name: Ploy
summary: AI website growth platform that monitors traffic, competitors, SEO, ads, and conversion gaps, then uses agents to write content, fix site issues, and run experiments.
drprepper: Strong fit for DR Prepper lead-gen and fulfillment landing pages once analytics are connected; useful because it targets measurable conversion and visitor-intent work, not just copy generation.

aluma: Could tighten Aluma lead-gen pages if they move to a GitHub/Vercel workflow with PostHog-style analytics.
next: Evaluate on a non-critical page only; require human PR review and confirm analytics/rollback behavior before any production use.
name: Velyr
summary: AI growth agent for React, Next.js, and Vite sites that finds conversion issues and opens GitHub pull requests with Vercel previews, human approval, and rollback checks.
drprepper: Good candidate for DR Prepper landing-page and quote-flow CRO once analytics are wired; useful because it ships reviewable code instead of just another report.

name: RoundtableSpace — Claude Mythos animated websites
summary: Workflow: Claude strategy/copy → AI-generated visual assets → Lovable/site builder → scroll animation → polish.
drprepper: Potential recipe for a premium fulfillment page if kept practical and trust-focused.
aluma: Could create educational construction landing pages or proposal visuals.
next: Use as reference after the DR Prepper conversion structure is written.

name: Artificial Corner — Claude stack list
summary: Thread about things worth installing in Claude: humanizer, social-media skills, marketing skills, Google Drive/Zapier/Notion MCP, repomix, etc.
drprepper: Potential stack for content, CRO, Google Workspace workflows, repository summaries, and marketing execution.
aluma: Useful for prospect research and outreach automation if integrated carefully.
next: Do not install blindly; evaluate only the pieces tied to active workflows.

name: ui-skills.com
summary: Directory of design-engineering skills: baseline UI, accessibility fixes, motion performance, frontend design, WCAG audits, React doctor, shadcn, animation principles.
drprepper: Use as agent guidance for frontend polish, accessibility, and motion performance on DR Prepper/PrepShip surfaces.
aluma: Useful for clean, credible lead-gen page QA.
next: Borrow baseline-ui, frontend-design, accessibility, and motion-performance checklists.

aluma: Potentially useful if Aluma tooling grows into a larger codebase that needs architecture maps or onboarding docs.
next: Already installed for Codex skills; test on a non-production repo and review generated dashboards for accuracy before relying on them.
name: Understand-Anything
summary: Open-source plugin/skill set that combines LLM analysis and static analysis to build interactive dashboards and knowledge graphs for codebases.
drprepper: Useful for onboarding into PrepShip/client-portal code, explaining diffs, and making large repos easier to audit before changing fulfillment or billing logic.

name: RoundtableSpace — AI landing pages look the same
summary: Tutorial/signpost on avoiding generic AI-made landing pages through references, rules, typography, spacing, hierarchy, and design docs.
drprepper: Best immediate lesson: define design rules before generating hero sections. Prevents flashy but low-trust fulfillment marketing.
aluma: Useful for building credible lead-gen pages that do not look like generic AI SaaS.
next: Create DR Prepper landing-page DESIGN.md and conversion checklist.

aluma: Could support structured outreach/nurture sequences for contractor and project-intel leads if list quality and send permissions are handled carefully.
next: Test only with a small internal or opted-in list first; define approval rules, unsubscribe hygiene, and human review before any customer-facing sends.
name: EmailFlow AI
summary: AI email platform for creating, sending, and automating revenue-focused email campaigns and newsletters.
drprepper: Useful for practical ecommerce follow-up: welcome flows, reorder reminders, 3PL lead nurturing, and newsletter experiments without starting from a blank campaign.

aluma: Could help organize bid follow-ups, vendor replies, and project-intel emails if sensitive threads are scoped carefully.
next: Sandbox only with a low-risk inbox or label first; require privacy review and no-send-without-review rules before customer-facing use.
name: Dirac
summary: AI inbox agent that prepares a morning brief, triages email, and drafts replies for human approval rather than sending automatically.
drprepper: Useful candidate for DR Prepper, PrepShip, and KF Goodies sales/client inbox cleanup, follow-up drafting, and preventing important replies from slipping.

aluma: Could help organize bid follow-ups, prospect replies, and meeting prep for concrete/formwork outreach.
next: Do not connect inboxes blindly; first define email permissions, approval rules, VIP handling, and no-send-without-review guardrails.
name: Slashy
summary: AI email and calendar assistant with inbox drafting, labels, automations, task tracking, meeting prep, search, and MCP access for agents.
drprepper: Potentially useful for sales follow-up, client communication cleanup, task capture from emails, and keeping fulfillment/customer replies from slipping.

aluma: Indirect relevance only: monitor as a freight-market signal, not an Aluma sales tool.
next: Use in outreach to Amazon/Shopify sellers who need non-Amazon backup, local Gardena support, or exception handling that a platform freight product will not customize.
name: Amazon Supply Chain Services — LTL expansion signal
summary: Amazon opened less-than-truckload freight to businesses of all sizes, expanding its logistics pitch beyond Amazon-only sellers.
drprepper: Sales wedge: pitch DR Prepper as the local, human alternative for inbound freight handoffs, storage, FBA prep, and exception handling when sellers compare Amazon logistics options.

aluma: Useful only if Aluma-related spend or subcontractor paperwork starts flowing through a shared inbox that needs accounting capture.
next: Run a privacy/security review and test on a low-risk inbox label before connecting finance mail broadly; never auto-pay or auto-send without review.
name: DocStreamAI
summary: AI workflow that watches inboxes for invoices and receipts, extracts the data, and syncs it to QuickBooks or Xero.
drprepper: Strong fit for AP cleanup across DR Prepper, PrepShip, and KF Goodies: fewer missed receipts, faster bookkeeping handoff, and less manual invoice transcription.

name: MotionSites
summary: Swipe library of animated landing pages and prompts. Useful as reference material for a DR Prepper animated fulfillment landing page.
drprepper: Build a more compelling hero/process section: order received → picked → packed → shipped. Use as inspiration, not production code.
aluma: Possible reference for construction project/intel landing pages, but lower priority.
next: Collect 3–5 reference sections and convert into a DR Prepper DESIGN.md brief.

aluma: Could adapt the website-audit workflow for contractor, supplier, or project-intel prospecting, but lead quality needs checking.
next: Run a small manual batch and review lead quality/outreach tone before automating; avoid generic spam pitches.
name: ClientJam
summary: AI lead-generation tool that audits local business websites, scores the opportunity, and drafts tailored outreach emails.
drprepper: Useful pattern for finding DTC/ecommerce or local brands with weak websites and turning audits into practical 3PL or fulfillment outreach.

name: Reddit r/3PL lead signal
summary: Screenshot showed a potential high-volume 3PL/shipping lead discussing 1,000/day campaign work and carrier rates.
drprepper: Qualify softly: determine if they need full fulfillment, last-mile carrier rates, or campaign logistics support.
aluma: No direct fit.
next: If URL is available, add it here and use a soft public reply + DM.

name: Local newsletter AI-agent idea
summary: Idea around AI agents producing local newsletters. Better used as a lead-gen machine than a standalone side hustle.
drprepper: Create an Ecom Fulfillment Brief to attract brands and surface fulfillment pain points.
aluma: Create a weekly construction project/intel brief for Southern California concrete/formwork leads.
next: Prototype one issue template before automating.

aluma: No direct fit; useful only as an example of policy-change monitoring for sales timing.
next: Build a short prospect list of SoCal Amazon sellers advertising Prime/SFP or multichannel fulfillment before the July deadline.
name: Seller-Fulfilled Prime speed rules — July 2026 signal
summary: Amazon is raising Seller-Fulfilled Prime delivery-speed thresholds starting July 6, 2026 and adding zip-code-level delivery promises later in the year.
drprepper: Outreach trigger for Prime/SFP sellers: offer overflow storage, SLA-safe shipping, FBA prep, FBM backup, and human escalation before delivery metrics slip.

aluma: Possible fit for structured follow-up/reminder workflows after opt-in and compliance requirements are defined.
next: Benchmark against Twilio on deliverability, support, carrier fees, 10DLC setup, and WhatsApp needs before choosing.
name: Telnyx Messaging API
summary: SMS/MMS/RCS and WhatsApp messaging API that can be a lower-cost alternative to Twilio; it does not provide normal iMessage blue texting.
drprepper: Candidate for transactional or support messaging where cost control matters and the team can handle API setup and compliance.

aluma: Useful for opted-in prospect follow-ups or appointment reminders, not for unapproved cold outreach.
next: Compare against Telnyx and Sendblue for channel requirements; account for A2P/10DLC, carrier fees, WhatsApp Meta fees, and human-send approval.
name: Twilio Messaging
summary: Mature communications API for SMS, MMS, and WhatsApp; Apple Messages for Business is not the same as normal consumer iMessage blue texting.
drprepper: Reliable default for transactional SMS, WhatsApp support, order/status notifications, and customer follow-up once 10DLC and review rules are in place.

aluma: Could support warmer follow-up where prior context or consent exists; not appropriate for uncontrolled cold blasting.
next: Run a controlled deliverability/compliance test; define opt-in, unsubscribe, approval, and no-auto-send rules before production use.
name: Sendblue
summary: Messaging API focused on consumer-style iMessage/blue-bubble texting with SMS/MMS/RCS fallback; distinct from Apple Messages for Business.
drprepper: Best-fit provider to test if the goal is blue-bubble customer or prospect conversations, but only with opt-in, compliance, and deliverability guardrails.

aluma: Could help with multi-channel nurture sequences, but only after lead quality, consent, and outreach policy are clear.
next: Verify channel support, contact-based pricing, carrier fees, and compliance workflow before any real send volume.
name: Sent.dm
summary: Omnichannel messaging platform/router for SMS, MMS, RCS, and WhatsApp; no official consumer iMessage support was found in the comparison.
drprepper: Worth watching if DR Prepper needs routed campaigns or multichannel messaging beyond a basic SMS/WhatsApp setup.

name: Higgsfield AI MCP
summary: Claude + Higgsfield workflow for generating motion assets and scroll-driven product visuals from a brand kit and business context.
drprepper: Potential for custom motion clips in a warehouse/fulfillment landing page or onboarding explainer.
aluma: Could generate animated construction/formwork visuals for proposal collateral.
next: Use only after copy, offer, and conversion flow are locked.

aluma: Could capture estimator/project conversations and turn them into follow-up tasks without exposing sensitive project details to a generic meeting bot.
next: Verify local/private processing claims and export format before using it for sensitive customer, pricing, or project conversations.
name: Synopsule
summary: Private Mac/iPhone meeting transcript app for capturing call notes without relying on a cloud meeting bot.
drprepper: Useful for vendor calls, client onboarding, warehouse SOP discussions, and PrepShip requirements capture where a visible bot would be awkward.

aluma: Could capture estimator, vendor, and project-intel calls into reviewable notes while keeping sensitive job details off generic cloud meeting bots.
next: Test export quality on a low-risk call; verify local storage, consent rules, and redaction workflow before using it for customer, pricing, or project conversations.
name: Trace
summary: Mac app for private, on-device meeting transcripts with shortcut-based flagged moments; no meeting bot, account, or cloud recording required.
drprepper: Useful for supplier calls, client onboarding, warehouse SOP discussions, and sales follow-ups where a bot in the meeting would be awkward.

name: pulkitxm/claude-directory
summary: GitHub directory of experimental landing pages, hero sections, and interactive prototypes generated with Claude Fable 5. Includes prompts and code.
drprepper: Reference prompts and implementation patterns for cinematic hero/landing sections.
aluma: Reference library for niche project-intel or sales pages.
next: Inspect prompt.md files; never ship raw vibe-coded dependencies without review.

aluma: Strong pattern match for quote-heavy construction and supplier workflows where scope, price, capacity, and lead time need normalization.
next: Watch or test only with non-sensitive sample RFQs; verify privacy, pricing, and supplier quality before using for real project data.
name: Covari
summary: Manufacturing RFQ and sourcing workflow for uploading part packages, matching vetted suppliers, and comparing quotes by price, scope, and lead time.
drprepper: Useful as a model for structured quote intake and supplier comparison around packaging, warehouse supplies, or operational purchasing.

aluma: Could support field/admin follow-up capture after calls or site visits if sensitive project details stay out of unreviewed automations.
next: Verify Product Hunt source details and test with a low-risk inbox/calendar only; require no-send-without-review and no customer-facing actions until permissions are explicit.
name: VoiceOS
summary: Voice-controlled AI assistant for Mac/Windows that turns spoken instructions into app actions, dictation, replies, scheduling, and task capture.
drprepper: Useful candidate for hands-free admin: drafting replies, calendar scheduling, quick task capture, and repetitive email/calendar actions during warehouse or sales work.
